entry.title: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
Hello Community! 

Is it possible we haven't started a discussion yet about your favorite books, or the "book that changed your life," or the type of books you enjoy reading⁉️

I know it's impossible to pick just one favorite book, but I'm looking for recommendations for my next read.

Right now, there's a book that changed my life, but after reading it three times, the third version of myself might not recommend it as highly, haha 😂

Your Erroneous ZonesYour Erroneous Zone

This book helped me take more responsibility for my choices and stop blaming external environments or situations.

I usually alternate between reading classic books and "self-help" books. 
One of my favorite classics is:

1985 - George Orwell

I don’t think this book needs much explanation. If you haven’t read it, you definitely should. It's an incredible book. I read it in my native language because it was too difficult for me to read in English.

But my favorite author overall, the one who got me into reading, is:

Herman Hessen

I love how he blends psychology with narrative. Siddhartha, Demian, Narcissus and Goldmund—I love them all. 

Special mention to another author:

Yuval Noah Harari

He wrote best-selling books that introduced me to history (which used to be one of my least favorite subjects in school 😅). Well written and a pleasure to read

Anyway, enough about me—I’m really curious to hear about your favorite books and authors.  I’m always on the lookout for something new to read, so hit me up with your suggestions!
What books have made a difference in your life, or just ones you really enjoyed?

Can’t wait to see what everyone’s into!

entry.title: Focused Run Post Installation
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We recently installed Focused Run system in our environment. As per the master guide, we are not sure about Post steps.

Can we get documentation or steps for Post process. Like do we need get connect our system to cloud ALM for applications to work?

Also when we run T code : FRUN, not able to see any Tiles on the page.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Focused Run Post Installation
entry.content:
The Master Guide also has information on post installation activities (from section 4.7 - guide for FRUN 4.0 FP03 (latest)).

I recommend completing these steps if you have not already. However to supplement these steps, the Expert Portal provides additional information on application side step ups, supported products and also preparing the infrastructure post installation (see heading "Infrastructure Administration")

If you want to do monitoring of some Cloud Services, then you will need to connect FRUN to Cloud ALM. See heading "Monitoring Cloud Services through SAP Cloud ALM" in the same link above.

Finally, in regard to the Fiori Tiles. This could be for a number of reasons:

  • Permissions: https://help.sap.com/docs/FRUN/6ba510f3be2945e19e497bfb1065b022/3f2ab0ec535d4143826718864e96898b.html?q=fiori
  • Use of HTTPS/Web dispatcher: 3434823 - SAP Fiori Launchpad empty after system update 
  • Known issues:
    • 2423019 - Cannot find an app's tile in Fiori Launchpad - SAP S/4HANA
    • 2782173 - Tile missing on Fiori Launchpad

I recommend also checking latest release SAP Notes for your FP version in order to ensure an known issues can be addressed.

entry.title: Re: SEVT TABLE DELETING OLD DATA
entry.content:
Hi LuisGuilherme 

There was may be some issue before, As if event sender is working fine it should automatically disappear 10 records every 3 second.If records are not moving from SEVT then there must be some issue.

The table "SEVT" contains the log of all steps or changes made within the SAP Business One, that is, each time you update a record either a document definitions, master data or user tables in this table creates a log of that change, but just who did it, when he did as object code or registration was made.

Content of the SEVT table can be truncated if you are not requiring to have the generated B1 events handled(If the integration framework is not using any scenarios, then we can completely deactivate the events creation in the B1 environment).We always recommend to create a backup On SBOCOMMON database before executing the deletion task.

If the SEVT table is having many entries, then it’s indicating as the Event Sender Configuration is not correct. Once the integration framework is installed, it enables the event collection via the table SEVT, and if those are not handled correctly by EventSender, then this is leading to the reported issue (huge records in SEVT table). If the EventSender is stopped, then it would not solve anything, the event will be generated.

Please configure the EventSender properly. If you will encounter some issue, please refer to the SAP note 1882000 as well.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Memoirs from The League of Storytellers
entry.content:
It is March 2024, and a motley crew of SAP colleagues are brought together by the League of Storytellers program; a thrilling learning adventure that promised to empower them with the skills and knowledge to become storytellers for SAP. This includes the tools necessary to host tours in the SAP Discovery Showroom, that is a physical, digital, and virtual multimedia experience for employees, customers, and partners.  Fast forward to 6 months later and the group is brought back together to celebrate the end of their rotation and reminisce about the journey they have been on. Here are the thoughts of four members, who share their experiences and some nuggets of wisdom: 

Hanna Tsatsopitas 

Working Student, Discover SAP 

Hanna TsatsopitasI learned a lot of new topics and content which I was not as experienced in before. I also gained presentation skills through the enablement sessions. I am typically a shy person, and I challenged myself to speak before an audience, which I enjoy. 

At the start of the program, the learning material and enablement sessions can seem like a daunting task. Do not be intimidated. You do not have to know everything by heart. It is important to ask questions to a buddy you will be paired with, as well as reaching out to others. Be present at the enablement sessions because they are helpful. In summary, great insights, clarity and presentation skills. 

 

Jennifer SpannagelJennifer Spannagel

Internal IT, Business Operations.  

My daily job involves making sure everyone SAP colleague understands the digital workplace. My role involves both marketing and communication explaining the nuts and bolts of the digital workplace. Ideally, my team creates material like blogs, newsletters, videos and training to bring out the best work in every colleague. 

I got in touch with the League of Storytellers during the Experience Technology open house in February 2024. I believed it was a good opportunity to expand my network and to learn more about what Corporate Processes and IT does at SAP. I was curious and wanted to get in touch with experts from the other modules and units, just as I am an expert in the digital workplace and how we work at SAP. I also wanted to advance my public speaking skills, because I had started exploring moderating and hosting roles. I wanted to stretch my boundaries and do something outside my comfort zone. 

I enjoyed the enablement sessions. We had a very nice group and comprehensive onboarding by Britta Lehn and Anja Rosker and their team. We got a good grasp of the important skills to put in use when giving tours in the showroom to different audiences: Customers, partners, colleagues. I also enjoyed being part of a versatile group of colleagues from different backgrounds.  I also enjoyed being part of an on-site, after years of working remotely since the pandemic.  

Take a chance and try. The SAP Discovery Showroom feels like a lounge with that homely, intimate atmosphere. And once you get into the program, embrace it, be present, bring the best of yourself and take the best of others to expand your knowledge.  

 

Michael Schmidt 

S2P (Source to Pay) / Strategic Procurement 

I have been at SAP for 26 years. In my earlier roles, I did some consulting work and training in the field, and then I moved to internal IT, working within the procurement area. 

Part of the reason I registered for the program was that I like to interact with people. I did a lot of customer training and workshops in the past. Being an instructor who speaks in front of people, you need to prepare and to extend your view, looking beyond your own nose. So, this program was an interesting way for me to do something different. 

I liked the program. I think it was good and well organized. I would say that owing to experience, I knew the material, but it was a good foundation. I could check the content and contribute to it as well.  

To any hopeful future League members, if you are interested in networking and extending your knowledge, or simply connecting to people, give it a try. I got in touch with interesting colleagues, which was great for me. 

Christian Philipp Walter 

Working Student, Experience Garage. 

Christian Philipp WalterI considered what the program would be for me before enrolling; I thought it would be a nice challenge and would help me build some invaluable public speaking experience. I wanted to experience talking to people and audiences and to get more comfortable at it.  

The enablement sessions were well-structured. I had a buddy I could call and ask questions. I received a good brief every time, and I understood how storytelling works. The enablement covered an extensive list topics, including soft skills, presentation skills and public speaking etiquette. My first presentation in the showroom was fun. I met different people and gave them an experience of how things work at SAP, from my point of view. Personally, I liked the module about Future-proofing SAP because of my work at Experience Garage; my team’s focus is people driven innovation. The topic felt closer to home. 

My advice? Go ahead, it is the best thing you can do for yourself, and the experience is great for your career and daily tasks.

entry.title: How to read XML string passed as input parameter to HANA stored procedure ?
entry.content:
I am trying to read a XML string passed as input parameter to a stored procedure and convert the values in the tags to a table variable. I am able to successfully parse XML to table if I use hardcoded string as below but I am not able to figure out how to supply input parameter XMLSTRING to inbuilt XMLTABLE() function.

Please help.

https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-sql-reference-guide/xmltable-function-string

 

PROCEDURE "sp_timesheetdata"(
    IN  XMLSTRING VARCHAR(5000)
)
   LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
   SQL SECURITY INVOKER
   --DEFAULT SCHEMA <default_schema_name>
   READS SQL DATA AS
BEGIN
DECLARE tempTable TABLE(ID VARCHAR(50), END_DATE VARCHAR(50), WORKORDER VARCHAR(50));

INSERT INTO :tempTable(ID,END_DATE,WORKORDER)
SELECT ID,END_DATE,WORKORDER FROM
XMLTABLE('resultset/row' PASSING  '<resultset>
<row>
<ID>12345</ID>
<DATE>2024-08-30 00:00:00</DATE>
<ORDER>273498</ORDER>
</row>
  <row>
  <ID>56789</ID>
  <DATE>2024-06-21 00:00:00</DATE>
  <ORDER>60657</ORDER>
  </row>
</resultset>'
   COLUMNS
   ID VARCHAR(50) PATH 'ID',
   DATE VARCHAR(50) PATH 'DATE',
   ORDER VARCHAR(50) PATH 'ORDER'
   ) as XTABLE;

   SELECT * FROM :tempTable; -- used in joins further

--
--

How to refer :XMLSTRING input parameter after keyword PASSING above ?

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Execution of Services (aka "Dual Order")
entry.content:
@Henrik_Kiessler

Hi Henrik,
excellent stuff.

Just having one question since you wrote "On the Executing Order all billing types and supported revenue recognition methods are possible".
Does this mean that in fact in the executing company a resource-related billing (DIP) is possible when there an execution order item / maintenance order comes into play?
If so, this would mean that dynamic costs can be charged by the executing company to the commercial
company. There the fixed-price item can only be charged to the customer via BDR. In real life that fixed price could be changed when necessary.

Is my understanding correct?

Thanks and BR
Harald

entry.title: Re: Fieldglass-Hyperscaler technical checklist
entry.content:
Hi Tina

Which type of certificates are you referring to? For browser access or system to system integrations? Please see the info below.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Customers access the SAP Fieldglass application over the Internet using the secure TLS protocol. As part of the protocol, digital certificates are used to encrypt the communication.

When SAP Fieldglass is deployed on the public cloud, the digital certificates for the server instances will be rotated every 30 days. This differs from the current implementation, where rotation was done yearly.

Browser Access

Customers who access the SAP Fieldglass application using a web browser do not require users to explicitly install SAP Fieldglass server certificates, as the browser’s certificate store will typically trust certificates issued by the “Let’s Encrypt” certificate authority. This may be dependent on IT policies / implementations, so it may be best to confirm with those teams, but action is typically not required.

System to system integration

Customers who use the system-to-system integrations (example: Web Services integrations) with SAP Fieldglass application should consult their IT departments / development teams to determine if their integration application requires the installation of SAP Fieldglass certificates to establish a trusted connection.  

SAP Fieldglass does not recommend that the customer store or install the SAP Fieldglass certificates (known as “Pinning”) because of how quickly they are rotated. If the customer chooses to store or install the SAP Fieldglass certificate, additional work is needed from the customer side to continue to access the SAP Fieldglass application without disruptions.

If the customer does not trust the refreshed SAP Fieldglass server certificate, then disruptions will occur in system-to-system integrations between the customer application and SAP Fieldglass.

entry.title: No Error for Serialized product confirming production task
entry.content:
Why system does not throw any error if no serial number is being entered for Serialized Product while confirming the production task?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: No Error for Serialized product confirming production...
entry.content:
The production task has a lot of flexibility, like the user can add input products and remove existing input products as well.

When the user provides the confirmation quantity and choose enter, the system automatically picks the input products whose confirmation method is backflush. However, when it comes to serialized products, system  always considers as explicit (even if in the production model the confirmation method is maintained as backflush), as the system cannot backflush the quantities by automatically determining serial numbers.

For serialized products, quantity gets confirmed and will be updated only if user adds the serial number to the input product. In Production Task, there are cases where user does not want to utilize some of the input products and continue the process and can confirm the remaining input products quantity.

Hence, for these scenarios, system does not display any error or warning messages.

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
Rules for Radicals: A Pragmatic Primer for Realistic Radicals is a 1971 book by American community activist and writer Saul D. Alinsky about how to successfully run a movement for change. 

The Rules

  1. "Power is not only what you have but what the enemy thinks you have."
  2. "Never go outside the experience of your people."
  3. "Whenever possible go outside of the experience of the enemy."
  4. "Make the enemy live up to its own book of rules."
  5. "Ridicule is man's most potent weapon. There is no defense. It is almost impossible to counterattack ridicule. Also it infuriates the opposition, who then react to your advantage."
  6. "A good tactic is one your people enjoy."
  7. "A tactic that drags on too long becomes a drag."
  8. "Keep the pressure on."
  9. "The threat is usually more terrifying than the thing itself."
  10. "The major premise for tactics is the development of operations that will maintain a constant pressure upon the opposition."
  11. "If you push a negative hard and deep enough it will break through into its counterside; this is based on the principle that every positive has its negative."
  12. "The price of a successful attack is a constructive alternative."
  13. "Pick the target, freeze it, personalize it, and polarize it."

entry.title: Re: Transport Custom Workflow from development to quality...
entry.content: There is sometimes a delay between transports and when you see the changes reflected in the target system. This is called (informally) the 'Cinderella Effect' - after midnight the under normal circumstances the HR buffers are reset. If you transport your workflow changes and then go to look for the changes and don't see them, try executing transaction SWU_OBUF. Also see https://me.sap.com/notes/0002890437

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition User Exit
entry.content:
Are you running S/4 or non-S/4 (ECC)? There might be 2 approaches here, depending on your System, the Nmbr of PR-Creators, etc.

Under normal circumstances, I assume you have maintained a number of Delivery Addresses via Trx. "MEAN". If you only have a limited number or PR-creators, you could consider creating new "MEAN"-records for a limited set of SAP-Users initiating those PR's, including their e-mail. That set of "MEAN"-records could be fetched in Custom Logic, for instance in User Exit "EXIT_SAPLMMDA_001" ... The UserExit is called in FM "MM_DELIVERY_ADDRESS_PROPOSE" and can be used to 'overrule' the Standard Address-proposals with your "SAP-ID"-MEAN ... You could experiment by putting a Breakpoint in the FM "MM_DELIVERY_ADDRESS_PROPOSE" and see what happens when overruling the "E_ADRNR" Output ... If this AddressNmbr however does not get passed / shown in the UserInterface, you will be generating an error of Msg-type "ME 083". Then I would suggest using BAdI "ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST" and its methods to influence the "ADRN2" field on the PR-Item level (there are "Getter and Setter" Methods for the Item data) ...

Using the BAdI and / or UserExit should get you a long way towards a solution (having also maintained "MEAN"-records for a limited set of PR-Creators).

On a S/4-System however, there are a number of new BAdIs that are interesting to investigate. In that case, I would focus on using the BAdI "MM_PUR_S4_SSPPR" (or "MM_PUR_S4_PR", preferably the first). SAP-Note 2950092 - Delivery Address defaulting in "Create Purchase Requisition" and "My Purchase Requisitions" offers info on the trigger / use of the BAdI and includes sample code to Address your Issue.

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Assigning to tickets to the last processor of the ticket
entry.content:
Hello,

PATH:BUSINESS CONFIGURATION > EDIT PROJECT SCOPE QUESTIONS > SERVICE > CUSTOMER CARE > SERVICE RERQUEST MANAGEMENT

1. In what business scenario, this scoping question makes sense?

  • If you want to assign an unassigned ticket to the logged in user who responds to (reply in interactions) the ticket.

2. What effect/consequence scoping this would have to the UI.

When the logged in user responds to ticket (reply an email or compose a new email or add a portal post in this ticket, then:

  • The user is the last changed by user of the ticket.
  • The user is the processor (assigned to) of the ticket.

3. System behaviour once this is selected

  • If the ticket already has an agent assigned, the system will not reassign it to the current user.
  • Only editing tickets e.g. adding notes or changing fields without reply to customer will not trigger this user assignment

***Note: We do not have any help center document with details, specific to this scoping question .

Hope this helps - If Yes, do mark it as answered.

Thanks and Regards,

Ankush Pashine

entry.title: Live Data Connection To Manufacturing Data Objects for Analytics
entry.content:
To create a live connection to the HANA DB of Digital Manufacturing Insights, the host URL and client credentials need to be provided. With release 2408 of SAP Digital Manufacturing, the tile for Initialize Embedded Analytics has been renamed to Configuration for Analytics. See What's New for 2408 https://help.sap.com/whats-new/983fc3e099074d1ea3ea6db6caf41678?Environment=Manufacturing%20Insights&locale=en-US

The application now exposes how to enable Single Sign On for embedded analytics, and in addition exposes the data needed for Live Connection. Host URL and client credentials can be copied or downloaded to be leveraged in the respective analytical tool. 

The Reset button allows to renew the client credential as needed.

To leverage the Live Connection in SAP Datasphere, the following steps need to be executed:

In your SAP Datasphere instance, navigate to the Configuration section via the System menu in the lower left of your Datasphere screen.

Navigate further leveraging the Manage Live Data Connection button in the middle of the screen.

Choose the “+” in the header section to create a new connection.

In the popup window, select SAP HANA on the left underneath Connect to Live Data.

In the next popup window, define a name for the connection, select connection type SAP HANA Cloud, enter host URL, user and password as provided in the connection details, and finally click OK.

Close all popups left open.

Next please assure that SSL certificates are uploaded to your SAP Datasphere instance.

The certificates are described at https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-data-access-guide/import-certificates-for-ssl-connections-to-remote-sources . You need to download the two certificates listed for SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database.

Just copy the links (starting with https://) into your browser and download from there.

Then switch to your SAP Datasphere instance and again navigate to the Configuration section via the System menu in the lower left of your Datasphere screen. Click on the Security tab. Use the “+” button to upload both certificates to the SSL/TLS Certificates list.

Next, navigate to the connections via the menu on the left upper side.

If you have multiple spaces maintained, select your space accordingly.

You will be navigated to the list of connections.

Choose the Create button in the menu above the list to load the available connection types. Search for and select SAP HANA.

 

In the popup to create a connection, choose category Cloud and enter host and port as provided in the connection details.

Select Authentication Type User Name And Password and enter user and password provided in the connection details. Choose the Data Access pattern you request and press Next Step in the lower right corner to proceed. 

Enter a business name and technical name in the net popup and press Create Connection in the lower right corner of the popup.

The connection will be created and displayed as entry in the list. You may select the entry and choose validate in the menu above the list to confirm successful connection.

You can now access the views exposed in the schema for the user provided.

You may check in the Data Builder for a new Data Flow whether on the left-hand side in the menu for Sources the connection is listed. Expanding the connection tree of the user shared in the connection details, you should see the list of views for the Insights MDOs that can be accessed.

entry.title: EWM Data Management in a Nutshell
entry.content:
During the implementation phase of an EWM project, many customers and partners tend to be heavily focused on the implementation itself, without paying sufficient attention to data management after go-live. This can have a significant impact on the system's long-term performance and may even lead to difficulties in meeting legal requirements.

Throughout the day-to-day warehouse business transactions, SAP tables are filled with various types of data, such as deliveries, warehouse tasks, warehouse orders, logs, change documents, and PPF actions. It is crucial to define a comprehensive methodology to determine:

What data should be avoided from being stored in the system? 

What data should be regularly deleted ?

What data should be archived for long-term storage and compliance purposes?

lets have quick look into the objective of Data Management: - 

  • Greater system availability through reduced data volumes in the database
  • Better use of resources unitarization 
  • Improve system performance
  • Support in complying with legal data retention requirements through more automatization and more complete data archiving and storage processes

Before Talking about what are SAP Methodology for Data Management in EWM, its essential to understand Management strategy and their KPIs toward Managing the data and retaining these data on the system, Retention time can be maintained in Customizing, That's a prerequisites 

Data Management in EWM consisted of the following: -

1- Avoidances: - 

This refers to the practice of avoiding the storage of data that is not important for the business to retain in the live EWM system. and the goal is to prevent unnecessary data growth a

2- Deletion: - 

From short-term perspective the data can be kept on the system in number of days for analyses and get deleted afterwards.

 

For Deletion & Avoidance Methodologies, in Operations Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2023 section 10.6.2 Extended Warehouse Management, describes all tasks that can be automated and recommended Frequency. It is important that you monitor the successful execution of these tasks on a regular basis. 

Example 

 

OPS_OP2023.pdf (sap.com)

Note: - 

Objects like Application Log, Change Documents, PPF and qRFC-trace/log Information, can be deleted and avoided, the decision on which Methodology to be chosen its back to management strategy and visions

3- Archiving 

Its refers to the process associated with copying data and supporting documents from an active system (SAP) to an external source which no longer required in the system from the database, and it can be retrieve later-on. 

Archiving its simple click and object will be archived, however there are prerequisites has to meet to be able to archive the object as following in addition to that there are some dependencies between EWM archiving objects which need to be considered for example Vehicle activities WME_VEH can be only archived if they are completed and the related TU activities WME_TU are already archived.

To find out more about what are depended please check this note 1830178 - Archiving dependencies between EWM archiving objects

 

Let us have Delivery archiving as example: -

Prerequisites: - 

  • Completion (DCO) status is completed (9).
  • Successor documents (FDO) are preprocessed Report: /SCWM/POST_PROC_DLV_SET_DOY.
  • Archiving (DAC) status has value Yes (X) Report: /SCDL/BO_ARCHIVE.
  • Assigned HUs are archived Archiving Object: WME_HU.
  • Assigned TUs are completed (CHKO): Transaction: /SCWM/TU or /SCWM/CICO.
  • Assigned VAS orders are archived: Archiving Object: WME_VAS.
  • ECC-messages and LIME-entries are completely processed.

Write : -  /SCDL/DLV_INB_ARCH_WRITE

The write program reads the data records of the documents to be archived from the database and records them in an archive file

Delete: - /SCDL/DLV_INB_ARCH_DELETE

The delete program deletes the data records from the database that were previously archived with the write program.

Read: -/SCDL/DLV_INB_ACRH_READ

The read program reads the archived data records. In the selection screen of the read program, you can select the documents to be archived via the selection criteria in Deliveries and Restrictions

To find more about write, delete, read and read Archiving procedures for all other objects please visit our SAP Help Data Archiving in Extended Warehouse Management | SAP Help Portal

Most important notes:-

2388483 – How-To: Data Management for Technical Tables

1065952 - Deactivating the PPF log (performance in EWM)

1975173 - Delete unnecessary PPF triggers

1567007 - Deleting entries from the QRFCTRACE table

1423066 - Optimization of the performance in EWM

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement Service can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

If you would like to learn more on how Premium Engagement Services can support you during your transformation of EWM and TM to SAP S/4HANA, have a look at SAP Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/transformation-to-sap-s-4hana-for-warehouse-and-transportation-management/ba-p/13560716 .

Best Regards

Ahmed Yasser

entry.title: Retrieving User and Group Information in SAP BI 4.3 : A Practical Guide
entry.content:
The Audit DB is designed to store records of events within the system, offering insights into information access, modifications, and interactions. However, retrieving details about users and groups is difficult from the Audit DB and requires access to the CMS database.

For your needs, I recommend using BI on BI (leveraging the CMS System database) to generate reports containing this information.

  • 2399962 - CMS database with new data access driver from BI 4.2 SP03 
  • Unlock the CMS database with new data access driver for BI 4.2

Getting Started with BI on BI :

To begin retrieving user and group information, you'll need to use the BI on BI provided by SAP. Here's a step-by-step guide to get you started:

 

  1. Locate the universe and samples in the installation folder: ..\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\Samples\bionbi
  2. Import the universe from the LCMBIAR file into your BI 4.3 system using Promotion Management
  3. In the Central Management Console (CMC), navigate to BI on BI > BI Repository Analysis > Web Intelligence Samples
  4. You'll see a list of Web Intelligence reports. Right-click on the "Sample-User-To-Group-Relationship-Details" report and select "View"
  5. The report will open in a new window. Change the mode from "Reading" to "Design" and click on "Edit Query"

Now that you're in the query editor, you can customize your query to retrieve the desired information:

 

  1. To get a list of all users with their assigned groups, add the following filter to your query: "Kind equal to user", this will ensure that only user objects are returned in your results.
  2. If you want to retrieve the list of groups for a specific user, add this filter: "Name in List UserName" , replace "UserName" with the actual username you're interested.

 

 Conclusion : 

By leveraging the CMS database through this Web Intelligence report, we can gain valuable insights into the current user-group relationships within your SAP BusinessObjects environment.

entry.title: SAP 分析云中编辑故事的小技巧
entry.content:
在使用SAP分析云（SAC）编辑故事的过程中，面对其丰富而强大的功能，新手用户可能会感到有些迷茫，难以迅速定位所需的工具。为了帮助您更快地熟悉SAC，以下是一些实用的编辑技巧，旨在让您的故事面板功能全面、美观大方。 

图表相关 

1. 跨多个统计图同步颜色 

有时当您更改了某个统计图的调色板后，会意外地发现其他统计图的颜色也同步发生了变化。这是因为默认情况下，用户更改某个统计图的调色板时，所有其他具有相同的颜色维或成员的图表，也会同步得到更新。例如以下屏幕截图的情况中，当我修改了右侧饼图的调色板（图片右侧），两张图表的颜色会同步更换。 

 

如果您不希望得到这样的效果，可以在颜色维度的更多菜单中勾选取消同步颜色。这会让两张表的颜色分配相互独立。（更多请参阅：更改调色板和跨统计图同步颜色 | SAP Help Portal） 

 2. 在统计图中应用排名 

有时图表中某个维的数据太多，很难在有限的空间内显示完整；或者只需要显示数据排名前几的成员的时候，可以应用排名。 

另外，SAC 还提供了“剩余组”的选项。“剩余组”，即除去“前/后N项”后其他成员的值的总和。可用于与“前/后N项”值进行对比。例如下图。 

请注意，版本维或层次结构维不支持“剩余组”。您会看到如下图的提示。 

如果您需要对分层维使用该功能（剩余组），则要将层次结构维更改为不分层显示。点开图表的生成器，点开对应维的层次结构选项，如下图，选择并应用不分层显示即可。 

（在统计图中应用排名 | SAP Help Portal） 

3. 显示数据之间的差异 

如果您想要突出数据不同版本或时间段的差异，可以选中统计图，在生成器面板的统计图加载项中添加差异。差异的显示形式有四种，分别为条形、数据标签、集成和瀑布。 

例如柱形图中，以时间作为维，可以根据自己的需求选择显示形式。 

请注意，并不是所有类型的图都支持差异。支持差异的图也不一定能支持全部四种显示的形式。 

另外，多个差异是可以叠加的，即一张图可以同时展示多个差异。（向统计图中添加差异 | SAP Help Portal） 

如果您只需要对比两个数据点，SAC提供了另一种快速查看差异的方法。您只需选中一个值，将光标拖动至另一个值上即可。此功能适用于“条形图/柱状图”和“柱形折现组合图”。 

4. 统计图加载项 – 参考线 

可以在图表中定义一个参考线，显示统计图上重要的值。分为固定参考线和动态参考线，分别需要输入一个固定的值，或参考的账户和聚合方式。还可以为参考线上方或下方的背景区域涂色。（向统计图添加功能 | SAP Help Portal） 

5. 统计图加载项 – 阈值 

阈值可以用于将度量的值与某个固定的范围或其他度量的倍数进行比较。可以快速发现哪些区域的数据正常，哪些需要改进。您只需选中相应的统计图，在统计图加载项中点开阈值，选择度量后可以开始编辑阈值——在第一个下拉框中可以选择与数字范围比较还是与度量比较。点击阈值的名称（默认为“新阈值”）可以重命名。 

若您选择与数字范围比较，请注意，图表上显示的数据标签可能自带单位，例如千、万或百万。在设定数字范围的时候要使用原始数据，单位为“千”就要多加3个0，“百万”就要多加6个0。如果0太多数不清楚，我们在面板最下方提供了可视化的带颜色的“数轴”。设置完毕后单击右下角“应用”。（创建故事定义的阈值（优化） | SAP Help Portal） 

应用后若图表未显示对应的颜色，则需要在图表的生成器中找到对应的账户，找到阈值图标，单击故事定义的显示阈值，如下： 

注意，这会覆盖原有的颜色。 

若您需要编辑此账户对应的阈值，也是在此处单击“编辑范围”，即可重新设定阈值数据和对应的颜色。 

6. 使用故事和页面筛选器 

  • 当您需要缩小分析数据的范围时，可以使用筛选器。页面筛选器有很多类型，在工具栏“插入”的加号菜单有“筛选器/控件”选项。我们通常使用的是输入控件，即工具栏的第三个图标。 

单击输入控件新建一个页面筛选器后，我们点开“更多”菜单发现选项很少。这不是一个操作失误，而是因为还没有给这个筛选器选择维。选择维后就会出现我们所期待看到的选项。 

我们通常希望一个输入控件仅仅控制页面上某几个选定微件的数据范围。而SAC默认情况下，页面筛选器会控制页面上的所有微件。此时我们只需点开“链接分析”，在面板中勾选“仅选定微件”即可。 

  • 另一种常用的是筛选器面板，即故事筛选器。它相应的按钮在工具栏的“查看”中。筛选器面板会应用到所有页面，显示在屏幕的左侧。 

7. 微件的显示顺序 

编辑故事时，常会遇到多个微件有重叠部分的情况，此时需要在样式设置面板中调整显示顺序，这个设置按钮可能有些难找。 

响应式页面的相关技巧 

  • 响应式页面可以适应不同屏幕尺寸的 SAP 分析云故事。但如果您同时又有将故事导出为 Powerpoint（16：9）的需求，过于灵活的比例变动会使得导出的图片无法适配 Powerpoint，会出现上下或左右的白边留白。此时，您可以在编辑故事时修改设备的分辨率至相似的比例。这样可以尽量减少导出后再修改图片比例的工作量。 

 

同时请注意，需要将故事切换至查看模式后才有导出的选项（在工具栏左上方的“文件”中）。处于编辑模式的故事是没有导出按钮的。 

 

  • 有的时候我们要跨响应式页面和画布复制组件，这里有个小技巧：因为响应式页面的微件大小（包括文字大小等）随屏幕分辨率改变，因此如果您发现微件从响应式页面复制到画布后偏大了，可以按Ctrl + ‘-’，缩小屏幕分辨率后重新赋值，会发现微件复制过去后统一变小了。若微件偏小，则同理放大屏幕分辨率。这会省去一大部分在画布页面逐一更改微件大小的繁琐步骤。 

 

总结 

本博客列举了一系列提升SAP 分析云使用效率的小技巧，随着技术的不断进步，SAP分析云也在不断更新和完善。我们期待在未来与你分享更多的技巧和最佳实践。感谢你的阅读，希望这些小技巧能够真正帮到你，让你在使用SAC时更加得心应手。

entry.title: Joule and SAP Business AI Now available for RISE with SAP Customers
entry.content:
SAP’s vision for Business AI is to bring powerful Generative AI capabilities across all our products and services through SAP BTP to benefit the customers in achieving measurable business outcomes. As you are already aware, SAP introduced Joule in Sep 2023, a copilot that truly understands your business revolutionizing the interactions with SAP systems making every touchpoint count and every task simpler in a secure and compliant way. Joule will be embedded across SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio, delivering proactive and contextualized insights from SAP solutions and third-party sources. Joule is everywhere, was one of the most discussed topics at SAPPHIRE 2024, and needs no introduction, find out everything you need to know about Joule on this blog.

We are excited to announce Joule is now available for RISE with SAP customers with significant enhancements to RISE with SAP commercial packages focused on accelerating digital transformation and bringing the power of AI to all our customers. Joule, our AI copilot, will now be available for all existing and new RISE with SAP customers, natively integrating into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Since launching RISE with SAP in Jan 2021, our mission to transform customers into a cloud operating model had a strong momentum in RISE adoption. We have helped over 6000+ customers across various industries and geographies to transform to a cloud operating model through RISE with SAP among which 3000+ customers went live.

During this journey of cloud transformation, we have observed that it is essential for every enterprise to evolve with the latest technologies in order to adopt them and remain competitive in the market. To maintain the pace with the need for agility and innovation, while moving towards fast-changing technologies, customers need to adapt to changes that are evolving in AI technologies and innovations. As AI and Generative AI have become the top priorities for every enterprise striving to stay competitive and maintain its market position, there are a few challenges that all enterprises must acknowledge and overcome.

Below are the transformational drivers that are impacting customers to be prepared for the future.

  • Rising Customer Expectations: In the digital age, customers are more empowered than ever, driving the demand for exceptional experiences, personalized services, and seamless interactions across various channels.
  • Technological Advancements: The rapid emergence of technologies like generative AI presents both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses to stay ahead by leveraging innovation for growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Enhancing operational efficiency is crucial for enterprises aiming to streamline processes, reduce costs, and boost productivity.
  • Competitive Pressure: To maintain a competitive edge and secure market position, companies must continuously evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape.

To address these challenges, SAP Cloud ERP is infused with AI capabilities to accelerate business ability and meet business goals.

McKinsey reports that Generative AI could generate over $2.6 to $4.4 trillion incremental value annually to the global market. As AI adoption accelerates, particularly in the area of Generative AI, there is an extreme need to capitalize on this technology. Leaders across industries are prioritizing investments and closely tracking the potential impact of Generative AI, recognizing that failure to stay competitive could pose significant risks. CEOs are making these strategic decisions to avoid losing market share and maintaining their competitive edge.

 

Why only cloud? And limitations of On-Premise:

The latest technological advancements like Generative AI need high computation power for efficient and accelerated performance. It requires a level of infrastructure that can handle the speed at which the innovations are changing and, run, activate, and deploy the AI use cases effectively. Data plays a critical role in implementing AI use cases, with the volume of data growing rapidly each day. To efficiently process and fine-tune this data for optimal and accelerated performance, the latest and most powerful infrastructure that can scale according to evolving requirements is essential.

The on-premise environments become challenging when scaling the systems to meet increased AI demand. The computation power for on-premise systems becomes costly and time-consuming. Significant resources and infrastructure are required to grow and maintain hardware and software systems. The on-premise environment presents significant challenges, making a cloud a more effective option considering scalability, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

Benefits to Customers 

By adopting AI, customers can gain benefits that can impact both their Business and People. In the field of business, AI enhances decision-making faster, with data-driven insights and recommendations, ensuring safer and more informed choices. For individuals, AI transforms work through automation and streamlines user experiences, ultimately driving better business outcomes

Benefits of AI for Business:

Reinvent Customer Service with personalized experiences that address customer needs quickly.

Boost Finance Excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.

Drive Operational Excellence by optimizing manufacturing and asset performance and elevating product quality.

Advance Intelligent Order Fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

Benefits of AI for People:

Empower End Users to increase efficiency and eliminate redundant tasks ​

Interact with Business using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.​

Uncover Insights that would otherwise be challenging or time-consuming to discover 

 

SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP

Aligning with SAP’s Business AI strategy, SAP launched the integration of Joule starting with Success Factors in Dec 2023. Now, Joule is available for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud for RISE with SAP customers. Joule offers a navigational pattern helping in navigating to relevant SAP Fiori apps and letting the user work directly in the app’s web UI for new and occasional users without prior knowledge of individual SAP Fiori apps.

RISE customers will have the advantage of experiencing generative AI capabilities across SAP cloud ERP. All existing and new RISE customers will have access to Joule to quickly access apps, effectively gain insights on business objects, and speedily resolve user issues. There are a few prerequisites to access Joule on SAP S/4HANA quoted below:

Prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA (requires administrator role):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or later.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, with the following entitlements
    • Joule Entitlements - Joule(das-application), please refer to license topic
    • SAP Workzone, standard edition
    • SAP Cloud Connectivity
    • Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Cloud Connector with Principal Propagation
    • System Mapping to expose the required HTTP protocol

To know more about the capabilities of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition and complete the configuration guide follow this link.

Commercial Packages

As part of RISE with SAP offerings, entitlements are provided with different customer profile packages like Base Edition, Premium Edition, and Premium Plus Edition. Joule is accessible in all available packages Base, Premium, and Premium Plus for RISE customers. Joule will be accessible by all existing and new RISE customers fulfilling the prerequisites mentioned above to use capabilities. Joule and Generative AI will be part of the new SKU as entitlements under the Artificial Intelligence domain.

RISE Base Edition: Provides small organizations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition functionalities with a lean setup

RISE Premium Edition: Enables organization’s business transformation supported by built-in AI capabilities into SAP applications, process automation, low-code business application development, and access to supplier networks.

RISE Premium Plus Edition: Extends premium by unlocking more AI scenarios, including sustainability solutions, and advanced finance capabilities.

AI has significant potential to accelerate business processes and optimize decision-making. Since AI is at the top of every enterprise’s priority list, we are constantly innovating to deliver more AI-powered business processes and bringing the capabilities of Business AI as an entitlement as part of the SKU for RISE with SAP. Generative AI capabilities are available for RISE Premium and RISE Premium Plus packages where respective AI units are available based on the package. RISE with SAP Premium customers are provided with AI units to pilot AI-driven innovations like SAP Cash Application, SAP Business Integrity Screening, and SAP Service Management.

SAP AI Unit is a single SKU. SAP’s AI use cases are commercialized as capabilities within a single SKU regardless of LoBs. All customers can license SAP AI Unit to purchase AI units but to be able to use specific AI capabilities, you need to fulfill the licensing pre-requisites.

AI units are like a virtual currency that allows you to consume a wide set of AI use cases across different LoB solutions within SAP which included Joule overages. AI units are based on annual subscriptions and are sold in a block of 100 AI units. The minimum block to purchase is 1 per year and must be used by the end of the year as all unused AI units will be lost if not consumed by the end of the year.

Commercials & Estimator

RISE customers will have entitlements of 2500 Joule messages per FUE per contract year free of charge to interact with SAP A/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Once the entitled number of messages is consumed and you are willing to continue accessing Joule, you can purchase additional messages with AI units SKU. A message is an electronic communication exchanged via the capability of cloud service. For cloud services like Joule, a user request, together with the corresponding response from Joule is considered as 1 message.

Customers can use the AI Unit Estimator to estimate x number of AI units required to run the selected AI capabilities and can request a quote from the estimator directly. After selecting the AI feature, the customer can add the number of transactions (in a block of 10,000) you would need for the selected AI feature. Cost estimation is automatically shown based on the selection and a quote can be created from the same page for the selected AI feature and the transactions based on the requirement.

Another way where customers can access the AI services is from the SAP for Me dashboard under the Portfolio & Products tab by selecting Business AI and activating the AI feature. The features are listed based on the SAP products they belong to, where customers can select whether to activate the feature or not. The customer has the option to view the consumption for the respective feature or calculate cost estimation for the feature by selecting an estimator for the respective feature.

Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP offers innovation adopter services to help you jump-start your projects with SAP’s latest products and solutions. SAP Early Adopter Care connects customers with products to help them simplify, innovate, and digitize fast. Through the support of the Early Adopter Care project coach, we enable two-way communication, and transparency, and allow stakeholders to minimize project risk.

Benefits of the EAC program:

Support with early implementation of SAP’s latest innovations.

Direct channel to development for product and solution insights.

To join the Early adopter care program for Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition, check out this link. Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition started on 15th July and is open till 31st Dec 2024.

Conditions for participation:

Participation in the early adopter care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following requisites.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01
  • Plans to use Joule in production
  • Willingness to become a reference
  • The full set of restrictions can be found here General Constraints for Joule & Constraints for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Customer Engagement Initiative: AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

What is in for you?

  • Opportunity to define and shape the product's future.
  • Gain early insights and actively engage to learn and shape the new innovations that matter.
  • Access and engage directly with the product engineering teams.

How to get engaged with us?

Visit this link and register your company for this opportunity.

 

Joule Integration – Step-by-step guide

To configure Joule and integrate with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, check the below blogs for a step-by-step guide.

Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
